channel.title: HoopsHype Rumors - San Antonio Spurs
channel.description: San Antonio Spurs trade rumors and free agency buzz from HoopsHype

item.title: De'Aaron Fox (questionable, sore ankle) is taking some …
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Tom Orsborn: De'Aaron Fox (questionable, sore ankle) is taking some shots during the portion of shootaround open to media. x.com

item.title: Devin Vassell on Victor Wembanyama: He's ready to go, but he must calm his emotions
item.description: [Source: Hoops Hype] Tom Orsborn: Devin Vassell tells reporters he knows Wemby is "ready to go": "That's what we need. We need an upset Vic who's ready to go and attack the game. We've seen it before. we've seen when Vic gets upset." At the same time, Vic must "calm his emotions," Vassell added. x.com

item.title: #Spurs just added rookie guard Dylan Harper to their …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: #Spurs just added rookie guard Dylan Harper to their injury report, listing him as questionable with left knee soreness. Harper was at shootaround this morning after recording 24 points, 7 rebounds and 3 steals in 27 minutes in Game 4.

item.title: The word at the practice facility after shootaround …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: The word at the practice facility after shootaround this morning is Fox will be a game-time decision. Spurs listed him as questionable yesterday with right ankle soreness after a collision with Ayo Dosunmu in Game 4.

item.title: "In terms of taking care of your body, he should be a …
item.description: After the 2-on-2, which included skill flashes from Wembanyama but a stark difference in strength between an adult and teenager separated by 11 years in age, Gobert took Wembanyama and Raynaud to the weight room. He gave them a glimpse inside the fitness program that has allowed him to be so durable. "In terms of taking care of your body, he should be a model for all big men," Wembanyama said.

item.title: "It's all the hours spent getting stronger, getting …
item.description: In Wembanyama, he found a rising talent that not only recognized Gobert's regimen but showed a great level of interest in emulating it. That meant a constant peppering of questions about nutrition, fitness and the secrets of injury avoidance. Gobert even said before the series that Wembanyama recently texted him to ask which kind of water filters he had in his house. "It's all the hours spent getting stronger, getting more flexible," Gobert said. "Your recovery, your balance. These are essential

item.title: "He's played a huge role in my journey," Wembanyama …
item.description: Gobert also happens to have Wembanyama's ear and admiration. Gobert is one of the greatest French basketball players ever and, considering their shared position and similar stature, served as an exemplar for Wembanyama, who appears on track to become the greatest hooper in French history. "He's played a huge role in my journey," Wembanyama said before the series. "[He] has been a role model, has inspired me in so many ways that should actually inspire more people."

item.title: "France, we have very unique type of fans. A lot of …
item.description: "I hope that the French people are proud," Gobert said. "France, we have very unique type of fans. A lot of times, sometimes there is this thing where they're harder on the French people than on others and they rather be fans of others. "What I truly care about is for the young kids in France that they have people to look up to and can see what is possible."

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs listed guard De'Aaron Fox as …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs listed guard De'Aaron Fox as questionable for Tuesday's Game 5 of the Western Conference semifinals at Frost Bank Center because of right ankle soreness. Fox was injured with 7:42 remaining in the third quarter Monday in a Game 4 loss at Minnesota that evened the series at 2-2. Timberwolves guard Ayo Dosunmu landed on Fox's right ankle while diving for a loose ball.

item.title: De'Aaron Fox downgraded to questionable for Game 5 with ankle soreness
item.description: The Spurs have listed De'Aaron Fox (left ankle soreness) as questionable for Tuesday’s Game 5 against Minnesota.

item.title: Stephon Castle on Julius Randle: He's very physical, it's a tough matchup
item.description: San Antonio Spurs guard Stephon Castle is one of the league's best defenders, often tasked with checking the other team's best player. For the Minnesota Timberwolves, that would be Anthony Edwards. While Castle has spent plenty of time harassing him in the first four games of the series he's spent a little more time on a less likely assignment. According to the NBA's matchup tracking data, the Wolf that San Antonio's second-year star has bothered the most is veteran big man Julius Randle. "He's

item.title: "I feel like the toughest thing with him is keeping …
item.description: "I feel like the toughest thing with him is keeping him off the glass and trying not to foul, you know, trying to combat his physicality," Caste said afterward. "He asserts, he hits first on offense. Just trying to withstand that without fouling is probably the toughest part."

item.title: There will be no suspension or fine for Victor Wembanyama after elbowing Naz Reid
item.description: Shams Charania: There will be no further discipline for Spurs star Victor Wembanyama after he was ejected for elbowing Naz Reid in Minnesota on Sunday night, sources tell ESPN. No suspension, no fine. Wembanyama will play in Game 5 against the Timberwolves on Tuesday night in San Antonio.

item.title: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama flagrant foul: Y'all have called for my career for less
item.description: Draymond Green: Y’all have called for my career for less.

item.title: I don't think it was intentional, but it was more like …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Harper said nobody wants to see anyone get hurt, but “you could see the frustration (from Wemby). I could see where he's coming from. I don't think it was intentional, but it was more like they keep grabbing me, I'm trying to protect myself cuz no one else going to protect me.”

item.title: Mitch Johnson on Victor Wembanyama: At some stage, he should be protected. And if not, he's going to have to protect himself
item.description: That’s black-letter NBA law, and even Mitch Johnson acknowledged it. But in perhaps his most fiery postgame comments as the Spurs head coach, Johnson called it “disgusting” that the pushes and bumps and hacks have been allowed to intensify without any extra whistles to go with them. “We don't complain, because we're just going to play,” Johnson said. “We don’t really give a (bleep), excuse my language. But at some stage (Wembanyama) should be protected. And if not, he's going to have to protect

item.title: "I'm glad he took matters into his own hands," Spurs …
item.description: Before being ejected, Wemby recorded four points, four rebounds, and three fouls in 13 minutes during San Antonio's 114-109 loss. The series is now tied 2-2. "I'm glad he took matters into his own hands," Spurs head coach Mitch Johnson said. "Not in terms of hitting Naz Reid, I want to be very clear about that. I'm glad Naz Reid is okay ... but [Wemby's] going to have to protect himself if [the refs] are not. "The amount of physicality that people play with, with him, at some level, you have to

item.title: Mitch Johnson says suspending Victor Wembanyama would be ridiculous
item.description: Tom Osborn: Mitch on possibility of the NBA suspending Wemby for Game 5: “There was zero intent…I think it would be ridiculous.”

item.title: "He's got a pretty impressive array of cuts and …
item.description: Matt Guzman: I asked Luke Kornet about the beatings Victor Wembanyama takes every game. He seems to always boast fresh scars and cuts. Here's what Kornet said: "He's got a pretty impressive array of cuts and lacerations. Obviously, he's got a lot of limbs. A lot of targets. The way he plays, he's always pretty cut up. It happens. He plays hard and aggressive, so obviously guys are trying to grab and grab him. He does a pretty good job of trying to play through it." #PorVida

item.title: His ejection with 8:39 left in the 2nd quarter is the …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: New record for Victor Wembanyama: His ejection with 8:39 left in the 2nd quarter is the earliest an All-Star has been ejected from a playoff game in the play-by-play era (since 1997-98), per ESPN Research. #NBA

item.title: Dylan Harper’s reaction to Wemby elbowing Naz Reid is …
item.description: Dylan Harper’s reaction to Wemby elbowing Naz Reid is priceless. 🤣

item.title: But rim was far more open to Timberwolves late. “He’s …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Anthony Edwards said it became “harder on us” without Victor Wembanyama on the floor because pace quickened and rest of team played loose. But rim was far more open to Timberwolves late. “He’s eight feet tall so, yeah, he gonna cover up the rim.”

item.title: Anthony Edwards explodes for 36 as Wolves even series vs. Spurs
item.description: Anthony Slater: Timberwolves survive a shaky final minute, take advantage of the Victor Wembanyama second quarter ejection, seal Game 4 with a handful of makes at the rim in the clutch. Anthony Edwards had 36 points in 40 minutes. Series tied 2-2 going back to San Antonio.

item.title: The NBA will review Victor Wembanyama’s flagrant-2 …
item.description: To clarify: The NBA will review Victor Wembanyama’s flagrant-2 foul tomorrow. It is standard operating procedure for all flagrant fouls. League discipline czar James Jones has discretion on whether more punishment (fine or suspension) is warranted.

item.title: That flagrant foul 2 on Victor Wembanyama marks his …
item.description: Alan Horton: That flagrant foul 2 on Victor Wembanyama marks his 1st career ejection

item.title: Victor Wembanyama ejected after throwing elbow at Naz Reid
item.description: Anthony Slater: Flagrant 2 on Victor Wembanyama for a swinging elbow to Naz Reid’s chin. He’s ejected early second quarter. Potential to swing the series.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama elbows Naz Reid in the face, he was …
item.description: CJ Fogler: Victor Wembanyama elbows Naz Reid in the face, he was given a flagrant 2 and ejected

item.title: Mitch Johnson on Spurs: 'I don’t think we’re anywhere near being a finished product by any means'
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch Johnson with reason for rest of NBA to worry: “We have a lot of young players I don’t think are anywhere near reaching their ceiling...And we’re still learning about each other. The coach has room to grow and get better. I don’t think we’re anywhere near being a finished product by any means”

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu jokes about what the Timberwolves’ mindset …
item.description: Chris Hine: Ayo Dosunmu jokes about what the Timberwolves’ mindset is tonight ahead of Game 5: “We coming out, we sending a message — nah, I’m just playing.” Later added that the Wolves were expecting Victor Wembanyama to not get suspended and play tonight.

item.title: De’Aaron Fox (sore ankle), Dylan Harper (sore knee) …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: De’Aaron Fox (sore ankle), Dylan Harper (sore knee) will be game-time decisions, per Mitch Johnson. "I know they're fighting, so if they can, they'll suit up," the coach said. "But got to make sure we save them from themselves also and make sure that they're capable of being out there."

item.title: NBA has announced Friday's Game 6 at Target Center …
item.description: Alan Horton: NBA has announced Friday's Game 6 at Target Center will be an 8:30 PM CT start -- technically an 8:42 tip with a possible 5-minute slide to 8:47.

item.title: Dylan Harper (knee) was going through his usual …
item.description: Jeff McDonald: Dylan Harper (knee) was going through his usual pregame warmup when I walked into the Frost Bank Center just now. Seems like a positive sign.

item.title: Prime Video continues its strong NBA postseason, with …
item.description: Prime Video Sports: Prime Video continues its strong NBA postseason, with YoY viewership gains and @spurs-@Timberwolves delivering the most-watched @NBAonPrime game yet!

item.title: "You're talking about the greatest coach pretty much …
item.description: For a team involved in a hotly contested second-round series with the Minnesota Timberwolves, the Spurs are grateful they have the five-time NBA champion coaching legend available to them as a valuable resource. That's especially true for playoff newcomers like Johnson, Bryant and Vassell. "You're talking about the greatest coach pretty much of all time to be able to sit here and tell you the experiences he's been through or what he's seen," Vassell said. "That's second to none."

item.title: "He helps out a lot," Vassell said. "The film session, …
item.description: Popovich, 77, retired from coaching in May 2025 after suffering a stroke five games into the 2024-25 season. The NBA's all-time winningest coach remains the team's president of basketball operations. Vassell indicated the assistance Popovich has provided the team during the Western Conference semifinals hasn't been limited to just individual conversations with players. "He helps out a lot," Vassell said. "The film session, he helped out a lot. He just has so much wisdom, and that's just stuff yo

item.title: "I can't give away all coach Pop's secrets," Vassell …
item.description: Vassell declined to go into detail about the "feedback" the team has received from the Hall of Famer. "I can't give away all coach Pop's secrets," Vassell said. "He gives us a lot, but just being more physical, being ready, playing off two feet. I'll just say that for now." Vassell said how much advice Popovich offers players individually "depends on the situation." "He has a relationship with everybody and everybody's relationship is a little bit different," the sixth-year pro said. "He might t

item.title: “I got a little bit emotional,” World Peace admitted. …
item.description: I spoke with Metta World Peace again earlier today, and he reflected on seeing Wembanyama escape the hammer that fell so hard on him fourteen years ago. His reaction was a mix of veteran wisdom, empathy for a young star, and a touch of lingering “what-if” regarding his own career. “I got a little bit emotional,” World Peace admitted. “Sometimes you gotta win on the court and focus on the game. I wish I could not be able to be suspended and focus on the game. But, they ejected him and gave him a

item.title: De'Aaron Fox, Dylan Harper good to go against Minnesota
item.description: Jeff McDonald: De'Aaron Fox and Dylan Harper both IN tonight, Spurs announce

item.title: Victor Wembanyama takes revenge as Spurs go up 3-2 over Minnesota
item.description: Victor Wembanyama set the tone early and never looked back as he led the San Antonio Spurs to a dominant 126-97 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves in Game 5 on Tuesday. Wembanyama tallied 27 points, 17 boards, and five assists to put the Spurs one win away from their first West finals in nine years.

item.title: Timberwolves get rolled in Game 5. They've lost last …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Timberwolves get rolled in Game 5. They've lost last two in San Antonio by 67 combined points. Shot below 40% tonight. Julius Randle is now 26-of-71 FG (36%) in series. Anthony Edwards (41, 40, 39 minutes the last three) can use the two-day break. G6 Friday in Minnesota.

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the days of Tim Duncan, but the Victor Wembanyama era promises to be the opposite. Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs.

item.title: Game 3 of the series Friday night averaged 5.25 …
item.description: Game 3 of the series Friday night averaged 5.25 million on Prime Video, marking the streamer’s largest NBA audience thus far (as of Friday). Viewership increased 24% from Thunder-Nuggets on ESPN last year (4.24M), and also notably surpassed Lakers-Thunder on Prime Video the previous night (5.08M). Game 2, as previously noted, delivered 5.2 million on ESPN last Wednesday — the most-watched game of the playoffs on the cable network. (Note that ESPN had aired only two second round games through Tue

item.title: Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the …
item.description: While some of the credit may belong to Anthony Edwards and the Timberwolves — who have played in highly-viewed games in each of their past two playoff runs — the Spurs account for several high water marks this season. Prior to the playoffs, the most-watched game of the season was Spurs-Thunder on ABC/ESPN Christmas Day and the most-watched outside of broadcast television was the Knicks-Spurs NBA Cup Final on Prime.

item.title: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs had a moment of silence for Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. Keldon Johnson came into the NBA at the same time as Clarke. "He seemed like an amazing person and I always heard great things about him," Keldon told me. "It sucks. Sending condolences to his family."

item.title: “It was 2 games ago. It's the playoffs…I was focused …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Asked Wemby if he had any regrets about the elbow after having time to reflect: “It was 2 games ago. It's the playoffs…I was focused on the game today and now I'm focusing on the game in Minnesota in 3 days. It's the playoff. We got to move on and I got to care about my team.”

item.title: "I feel like the rage-baiting would have been maybe …
item.description: The production inside marked San Antonio's second-most points in the paint in a postseason outing since 1998, when it scored 72 in its 2008 playoff opener, and the most by any team in a postseason contest against an opponent that featured Gobert, a four-time NBA Defensive Player of the Year. "I feel like the rage-baiting would have been maybe one of the strategies [used by the Timberwolves]," Wembanyama said. "I feel like I need to stay composed."

item.title: Devin Vassell said the team needed to see an "upset …
item.description: Spurs guard Devin Vassell said the team needed to see an "upset Vic" Tuesday night in Game 5 of the Western Conference semifinals, while San Antonio coach Mitch Johnson preferred to use the word "mature" in describing the approach Victor Wembanyama would need to use to bounce back from his first career ejection. Wembanyama, meanwhile, had his own thoughts. "I feel they ain't mutually exclusive," he said. "I'm looking for both."

item.title: Youngest player in NBA history with 25+ PTS
item.description: StatMuse: Youngest player in NBA history with 25+ PTS 15+ REB 5+ AST 3+ BLK in a playoff game.

item.title: "I feel like we got the Vic you've seen all year," …
item.description: "I feel like we got the Vic you've seen all year," Castle said. "I think his maturity level was off the charts. He played smart, didn't really foul much, took the shots that were there for him. When he's playing like that, playing aggressively, with everything he brings for us defensively, I feel like we're hard to beat. Just where his mindset has been for the last 48 hours, I know he wanted to finish Game 4. But not letting that get to him and coming out and playing like that was a big reason w

item.title: Anthony Edwards: 'Some of the stuff Wemby was doing you don’t really have too much of an answer for it'
item.description: ‪Chris Hine‬: Anthony Edwards postgame. On the slow start: “Some of the stuff Wemby was doing you don’t really have too much of an answer for it.” Then on breakdowns later in the game: “Game plan mistakes that we keep making, and it’s too late in the series to make these mistakes.”

item.title: DeMar DeRozan on Gregg Popovich: He tricked me into becoming a playmaker and it opened up the game for me
item.description: DeMar DeRozan: I think I didn’t realize it when I got to San Antonio. My mind was always like, ‘I got to shoot it thirty, thirty-five times. I’m getting after it.’ You know what I mean? That was always my mindset. When I got with Pop, Pop taught me how to use my teammates to make it easier for me later in the game. Utilizing them, playing a certain type of way, breaking down the game, understanding the game, sharing the ball — that opened up the game for me super easy. You know what I mean? And

item.title: Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs were a ratings drag during the days of Tim Duncan, but the Victor Wembanyama era promises to be the opposite. Sunday’s Spurs-Timberwolves second round NBA playoff Game 4 averaged a combined 7.9 million viewers across Nielsen and Adobe Analytics, trailing only Sixers-Celtics Game 7 on NBC following the Kentucky Derby (10.99M) as the most-watched game of the ongoing NBA Playoffs. The last non-clinching early round game to average as large an audience was Warriors-Lakers Game

item.title: NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial …
item.description: The Spurs announcer asks for a moment of silence for the recent passing of Brandon Clarke and Jason Collins. NBC inexplicably faded to black then cut to commercial in the middle of the moment; a DraftK*ngs commercial played on the broadcast.

item.title: “If he played for any other team in the league,” …
item.description: Dylan Harper just turned 20 in March. On Tuesday, he crossed the 250-minute plateau in his 10-game-old playoff career. And he’s already comfortable getting buckets on the big stage. Lookin’ just like his daddy. “If he played for any other team in the league,” fellow Spurs rookie Carter Bryant said, “he’d be starting and probably be winning the Rookie of the Year right now. And to see how he’s sacrificed and bought into his role, it’s amazing.” San Antonio has outscored opponents by 73 points wit

item.title: “He is the most complete player we’ve ever seen play …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Paul Pierce on Victor Wembanyama: “He is the most complete player we’ve ever seen play the game. Like, we can talk about Bron, Jordan, Joker but he is the most complete player in the history of the game.“

item.title: It’s hard to gauge whether the Rockets got the better …
item.description: How do you think the Kevin Durant trade played out for the Rockets? Was it worth losing players such as Jalen Green and Dillon Brooks? Metta World Peace: I think it’s played out the same way for both the Rockets and the Phoenix Suns. It was quite an even trade. It’s tough for the Rockets because Durant missed Game 1 with a knee injury, then injured his ankle in Game 2, causing him to miss more time. That hurt them a lot. I’ve been injured in the playoffs, so I know how it feels. I was supposed t

item.title: Dillon Brooks: Once Wemby gets his first MVP, you won't see anybody else get one
item.description: Brooks spoke about Victor Wembanyama’s incredible talent, saying that once the French star reaches his peak and wins his first MVP award, we might not see another player claim the honor until he retires. "Once Wemby gets his first, you probably won't see anybody else get one," Brooks said. "His numbers are gonna go up."

item.title: Brooks tells Ray that Victor Wembanyama hasn't reached …
item.description: Slatt♱: Dillon Brooks tells Ray that Victor Wembanyama hasn't reached his pull potential yet & he's the only one in the NBA who can guard him

item.title: “He’s been a big part of this whole year,” rookie …
item.description: It’s a common theme from everyone on the Spurs who has been mentored by Popovich this season in his new role after he retired from coaching last year. Current coach Mitch Johnson stepped in on an interim basis in November 2024 after Popovich suffered a stroke. After the season, Popovich held a news conference where he announced that he would step away from coaching and take on the new role of “El Jefe” (Spanish for “The Boss”), wearing a shirt that said as much. He is formally listed as the pres

item.title: Gregg Popovich appears to be turning the El Jefe role …
item.description: Gregg Popovich appears to be turning the El Jefe role into his own version of that, displaying his signature ability to forge relationships with players while easing up a bit on the throttle now that he is no longer in charge of managing the whole program. “It’s funny because you hear the stories about him being tough on guys and, obviously, he’s not my head coach, so I don’t kind of get that same experience,” Bryant said. “But I’m like, ‘What are you talking about?’ This dude is the sweetest, n

item.title: Kingston Flemings compares his game to Tyrese Maxey and De'Aaron Fox
item.description: So, what was the advice he gave you going into this experience? Kingston Flemings: Yeah, definitely just putting my head down and working and just being me at the end of the day. You can't be someone else. He had a talk with me during the year that I was trying to be to Allen Iverson. Don't be Allen Iverson, be Kingston Flemings. So really just being myself and if myself is going to be good enough for a team and when that team drafts me, I'm going to show them who I am. So that's really what coa

item.title: At one point near the end, you said you had no …
item.description: One week prior, as an "NBA Cares" ambassador, you had spoken to about 20 kids at a clinic in San Antonio on Final Four weekend there, and you had told them about everything from Sally Ride to the goodness of Jerry Sloan to your recent trip to Bhutan to teach basketball. At one point near the end, you said you had no championship rings -- 13 seasons, two NBA Finals, 830 games, 95 playoff games -- but, well, "I'll have a wedding ring in a couple months." The kids had cheered.

item.title: Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. the finalists for Social Justice Champion award
item.description: NBA: As we honor the sixth year of the NBA Social Justice Champion award, join us in recognizing Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. as the finalists for the 2025-26 season. This award honors NBA Hall of Famer Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and his lifetime commitment to driving impactful change around social justice.

item.title: Terrence Shannon Jr. added to injury report, questionable for Game 6 with head contusion
item.description: Timberwolves PR: UPDATED Minnesota @Timberwolves Status Report in advance of tonight’s Game 6 vs. San Antonio Spurs: QUESTIONABLE Shannon Jr- Head Contusion

item.title: Terrence Shannon upgraded to available for Game 6
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Shannon is available. That’s a big help for the Wolves

item.title: Wemby called Castle “amazing.”
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Wemby called Castle “amazing.” “The composure to make the late choice and to push the defense into as far as they can go to take care of all our strengths, he was great. He shot the ball and he should have.”

item.title: Victor Wembanyama (41 blocks) has the second most …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Victor Wembanyama (41 blocks) has the second most blocks in his first 10 playoff games only to Dikembe Mutombo’s 62 since blocks began being recorded in 1974.

item.title: Rudy Gobert on whether he's surprised by Wemby being …
item.description: Rudy Gobert on whether he's surprised by Wemby being so good this quickly: "I wouldn't say surprised...it's credit to his work. I'm aware of that. Sooner or later you get rewarded when you have that kind of mindset. He led his team. Now it's gonna be interesting to see how he's ready to face that next challenge and see if he takes his team to the promised land this year"

item.title: "I think this will be the standard. I mean, after the …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "I think this will be the standard. I mean, after the season's over," Dylan Harper says, noting that he didn't care about noise. "Kind of knew how good we were, and I knew the impact that everyone brought to the table, and I knew that we can do things like this."

item.title: Mberwolves won two games in this series and Spurs …
item.description: ‪Law Murray 📘‬: Timberwolves won two games in this series and Spurs still beat them by 97 points It's the most lopsided series win in Spurs franchise history And the 6th-largest series win by any team in NBA postseason history (3rd-largest after the conference quarterfinals)

item.title: "His gravity and how good he's got at setting up his …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "His gravity and how good he's got at setting up his angles for screens and forcing guys to have to either let the ball handler get downhill or get off his body... opens up the court for everybody, any defense is super worried about him... definitely a luxury." Steph on Vic

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: 'My mind is excited. My body is tired after this game'
item.description: Tom Petrini: "My mind is excited. My body is tired after this game... just the words 'conference finals,' it's crazy, like something I heard my whole life, and now being in it is just special. It's hopefully many more conference finals to come." -Wemby

item.title: Wemby on facing the Thunder in the WCF: "We know it's …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on facing the Thunder in the WCF: "We know it's gonna be a whole different series. This is a team that knows how to play basketball. They know their stuff. Will watch all their games and do the scouting and try to execute over there in Game 1"

item.title: It's going to be probably even bigger leap between …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "The nature of the playoffs makes it that we're going to play against better and better teams, and there was already a leap between the first and second round, and it's going to be probably even bigger leap between second and third, but we got good guidance." -Wemby

item.title: "Of course we're confident but we need to keep the …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby: "Of course we're confident but we need to keep the right confidence level. Right now I'm not even thinking about it. I'm just thinking about recovering, I'm thinking about getting this press conference over with it"

item.title: "They're the defending champs. They haven't lost the …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "They're the defending champs. They haven't lost the game in the playoffs, so we'll have to be at our best to make it competitive." -Mitch

item.title: Anthony Edwards on Spurs: 'They were the better team'
item.description: Ryan Eichten‬: Anthony Edwards: "I tip my hat to them. They were the better team."

item.title: “They’re the defending champs. We know it’s gonna be …
item.description: ESPN: “They’re the defending champs. We know it’s gonna be tough to knock them off but I think we’re pretty confident we could do it.” Stephon Castle on playing OKC in the Western Conference Finals 🔥

item.title: I'm about to go into another series healthy."
item.description: Williams has been sidelined since April 22 with a Grade 1 left hamstring strain, which typically carries a recovery time of 1-3 weeks. He has surpassed that window, all while OKC secured a second-round sweep of the Los Angeles Lakers without him. Williams was then seen at practice Friday, and he provided an update on his health status during a YouTube vlog he posted later that day. "I haven't had to rush back from my hamstring stuff at all," Williams said. "I'm actually taking extra days now the

item.title: Spurs eliminate Minnesota
item.description: After standing head-and-shoulders above the rest of the Western Conference all season, it will indeed be the San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder in the conference finals. The Spurs eliminated the Minnesota Timberwolves with a lop-sided 139-109 win in Game 6 on Friday, setting up a heavyweight fight to reach the NBA Finals. Game 1 of Spurs-Thunder will take place on Monday. The Thunder swept the Los Angeles Lakers in the other Western Conference semifinal.

item.title: Game 1: OKC vs. San Antonio on Monday, May 18
item.description: Justin Martinez: The OKC Thunder will face the San Antonio Spurs in the Western Conference finals. Here's the schedule, with tipoff times TBD: Game 1: OKC vs. San Antonio on Monday, May 18 Game 2: OKC vs. San Antonio on Wednesday, May 20 Game 3: OKC at San Antonio on Friday, May 22 Game 4: OKC at San Antonio on Sunday, May 24 Game 5: OKC vs. San Antonio on Tuesday, May 26* Game 6: OKC at San Antonio on Thursday, May 28* Game 7: OKC vs. San Antonio on Saturday, May 30* * If necessary

item.title: “What gives me confidence?… What we just did.”
item.description: ‪NBA‬: “What gives me confidence?… What we just did.” Wemby’s always been fond of the number 1. Now… ▪️ 1st postseason ▪️ 1st Conference Finals ▪️ Playing the 1-seed while trying to make 1st Finals Spurs will take on the Thunder in the WCF!

item.title: 30-point playoff games before turning 22: 2 — Stephon …
item.description: StatMuse: 30-point playoff games before turning 22: 2 — Stephon Castle 1 — Tony Parker 1 — Tim Duncan The only players to do it in franchise history.

item.title: Connelly will be entering the final season of his …
item.description: Connelly and Lloyd are two of the most respected executives in the league. Another season with them in charge should only help the Wolves’ efforts to close the gap on Oklahoma City and San Antonio, but there is work to be done. Connelly will be entering the final season of his contract, and Timberwolves ownership wants to get an extension done with him, team sources said. Finch has two more years left on his deal and will be back for his sixth full season next year.

item.title: Jaden McDaniels on Victor Wembanyama: 'He's the greatest defender I ever played against'
item.description: NBA Courtside: Jaden McDaniels says Wemby is the greatest defender he’s ever played against and is a big reason this series was tough: “Wemby is probably the greatest defender I ever played against. Him being so tall and so long, it was hard to get to the rim. That’s pretty much the most part. I was able to get to my spots but playing Wemby, he’s huge. He’s so tall. He’s the greatest defender I ever played against”

item.title: These very young @Spurs made it to the Conference …
item.description: Manu Ginobili: These very young @Spurs made it to the Conference Finals! Amazing work by players, coaches, staff, and everybody involved. Now let's go for more! #GoSpursGo

item.title: “We feel like we had a chance to beat these guys. But …
item.description: “I think they were the better team,” Wolves center Rudy Gobert said. “We feel like we had a chance to beat these guys. But when it came down to the court, they clearly outplayed us in three games. Now it’s about looking at why and understanding why.”

item.title: "I never seen that before," said Castle, who led the …
item.description: With eight minutes left and the Spurs ahead by 33 points, Timberwolves coach Chris Finch conceded defeat by pulling his starters, including Edwards, who finished with a team-high 24 points on 9-for-26 shooting. Rather than head straight for the Minnesota bench, Edwards made his way to the Spurs bench to offer his congratulations to coach Mitch Johnson and his players. "I never seen that before," said Castle, who led the Spurs with 32 points, a playoff-best 11 rebounds and six assists. "It was co

item.title: Naz Reid on how Wolves can compete with OKC, Spurs: We need to be less moody and more selfless
item.description: Dane Moore: With the Wolves getting knocked out by Oklahoma City and San Antonio these last two seasons, asked Naz Reid about what they need to do to be able to catch those teams. "Probably just the moodiness. You look at both of those teams and they're playing for one another, they're excited to be on the floor with one another, they're a team where they're selfless... I think we have more than enough talent... But just being less moody. I think that's just the name of the game for us, just bei

item.title: Fox takes pride in giving them little tidbits he calls …
item.description: “I think it’s dope and it’s something that I don’t take for granted,” Fox said in his postgame news conference. “Having guys who are this talented, have watched me throughout my career and then we get to be in the same locker room and get to be on the court together, it’s something I love. “And I want them to feel that success from as early as you can get in your career, because you never know when those things can come back around.” Fox takes pride in giving them little tidbits he calls a cheat

item.title: “They really don’t care who gets the credit. They …
item.description: For this trio, it’s only fitting that the one who appeared to be the worst shooter at the beginning of the year is pulling this off. Their whole brotherhood is founded on this genuine sense of not caring who gets to shine. “They really don’t care who gets the credit. They don’t care whose number is called,” Spurs wing Devin Vassell said. “It’s just great to be a part of it, because at the end of the day, nobody’s selfish. Everybody just wants to win.”

item.title: Alex Caruso on Spurs: 'They didn't just roll the ball out and get lucky and win 60 games in the NBA'
item.description: Justin Martinez: San Antonio has no shortage of talent, but I asked Alex Caruso how fundamentally sound it also is: "They won 62 games for a reason. They didn't just roll the ball out and get lucky and win 60 games in the NBA. That's a feat to be accomplished. You don't do that with just physical talent. There's some smarts that go into it and some IQ. They're a really well-coached team as well. Their bench has a lot of guys who've been around the league and know a lot of things about basketball

item.title: Asked Jaylin Williams how much he enjoys the challenge …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Asked Jaylin Williams how much he enjoys the challenge of OKC facing San Antonio: "I'm super excited. They're a competitive team. Every time they see us, they want to compete at a high level, so it's super fun. It's super fun to be able to compete against a really good team, and I'm looking forward to it."

item.title: Wembanyama expected to sit out France's FIBA commitments in July
item.description: With the San Antonio Spurs clinching a berth in the Western Conference Finals of the 2026 NBA Playoffs, presented by Google, Victor Wembanyama has been ruled out for the next FIBA window in July, to resume action in the FIBA World Cup 2027 Qualifiers. The head coach of the senior national team of France, Frederic Fauthoux, confirmed during a recent interview with RMC Sport that Wemby was extremely unlikely to be available if the Spurs progress.

item.title: Jalen Williams expected to play in Game 1 against Spurs
item.description: Justin Martinez: Jalen Williams is officially listed as available for Game 1 against San Antonio tomorrow. He has been out since April 22 due to a Grade 1 left hamstring strain.

item.title: “Honestly, nothing. The playoffs is such a different …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Jaylin Williams on what they can reference from their regular-season series with the Spurs to prepare for the WCF: “Honestly, nothing. The playoffs is such a different monster. It's a different game. You're never playing a team this many times in a row in the regular season.”

item.title: It’s a team we have a ton of respect for. We know what …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mark Daigneault on the historic implication of their Thunder-Spurs series: “It’s fitting because both teams earned their way here… It’s a team we have a ton of respect for. We know what they're capable of. It's an opponent that is incredibly worthy.”

item.title: De’Aaron Fox (Right ankle soreness) and Luke Kornet …
item.description: Jared Weiss: De’Aaron Fox (Right ankle soreness) and Luke Kornet (Left foot soreness) are listed as questionable for Game 1 of the Spurs vs. Thunder Western Conference Finals tomorrow night. Fox was questionable late in the Wolves series but played through it.

item.title: "Obviously a really good team. They've been right …
item.description: NBA Courtside: SGA on his upcoming match up with the Spurs: "Obviously a really good team. They've been right behind us all year so we obviously don't take them in the slightest. They're a really good team."

item.title: Subjectively speaking, the Thunder's (64-18) and …
item.description: Subjectively speaking, the Thunder's (64-18) and Spurs' (62-20) résumés make this the most exciting playoff series at least since 2018, when the 65-17 Houston Rockets attempted to take down the defending champion Golden State Warriors (who'd coasted to a 58-24 record in their second season with Kevin Durant). Objectively, this kind of clash is even more rare. The Western Conference finals will be the first playoff matchup this century, and just the seventh in NBA history, between teams with 62

item.title: The Thunder actually had a higher shot quality than …
item.description: The Thunder actually had a higher shot quality than the Spurs in their regular-season meetings, but their shotmaking let them down. On 3-pointers, the Spurs "should" have made 35.4%, according to GeniusIQ's shot quality metrics, which is close to their actual 36% accuracy. Yet while the Thunder "should" have made 37.2% of their 3s against the Spurs, based on factors such as defender distance and shooter identity, they hit 33%. Gilgeous-Alexander has to make more of his 3s in the conference final

item.title: Victor Wembanyama, Spurs to headline NBA’s Europe regular-season games in France and England
item.description: The NBA's two regular season games in Europe next January will be in Paris and Manchester and The Stein Line has learned that Victor Wembanyama's San Antonio Spurs — for obvious reasons — have been selected to headline the trip. Spurs vs. Pelicans, I'm told, is the expected pairing for the two-game series.

item.title: The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 …
item.description: Orlando and Memphis split this season's two Europe games in Berlin in London. Berlin and Paris are scheduled to serve as host cities for the two-game tour in 2028. The NBA, of course, continues to target the 2027-28 season as its launch date for a 12-to-16 team NBA Europe circuit that would be separate from #thisleague. Wemby and the Spurs played two games against Indiana in Paris in January 2025 on the NBA's last in-season visit to France.

item.title: "This is the Finals," a West assistant …
item.description: Hype for the NBA's Western Conference finals between the Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs has come from across the league. "This is the Finals," a West assistant coach told ESPN. "This has a chance to go down as one of the best series of the past decade," an Eastern Conference executive said. "This is the start of [another] Magic vs. Bird," a West scout said.

item.title: "Will the Spurs be able to guard the Thunder 1-on-1 …
item.description: "Will the Spurs be able to guard the Thunder 1-on-1 with the ball, and will the Thunder be able to guard Victor Wembanyama one-on-one with the ball? That will be huge. Whoever does that better will win," an East scout said. "I would try to start with Hartenstein, and end with Chet, and they'll be trying around with guys B, C, and D and see who can get into [Wembanyama's] body and be super physical and push him out to the perimeter, and they have three outstanding guys to do that with in Dort, Ca

item.title: De'Aaron Fox participated in shootaround after the …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: De'Aaron Fox participated in shootaround after the Spurs listed him as questionable for Game 1 due to his sore right ankle. Afterward, he indicated he'll be ready to go tonight: "Just going through the day, trying to test it out. I'll move my pregame up just to make sure."

item.title: “That is the strength of this team,” Daigneault said. …
item.description: OKC has so much depth that it has absorbed Jalen Williams various injuries just fine. He made his first All-Star game and became a key two-way player during last season’s championship run only to miss a combined 49 games this season to heal his right wrist and right hamstring. Williams could play in Game 1 of the Western Conference Finals against the San Antonio Spurs on Monday after missing the past six playoff games with a Grade 1 left hamstring strain. Yet, the Thunder have shown they can man

item.description: Starting Monday, May 18, NBA MVP Shai Gilgeous-Alexander’s defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder take on Victor Wembanyama’s San Antonio Spurs in the Western Conference Finals. Fans hoping to get in on the action to what pundits are calling “the real NBA Finals” can still grab last-minute tickets for all seven potential Thunder and Spurs home games. And when you play against those players, it's kind of an acquired thing, you know? You’re learning as you go. You can talk about as much you want, but you gotta develop a feel for it.”

item.title: “I was running down the court and was like ‘Yoooo!’” …
item.description: The shot was from a location on the Oklahoma City Thunder’s court that marked the 3-point revolution. He pulled up right near the spot of the double bang, the legendary Steph Curry game-winner in 2016 that informed the world the sport had changed for good. A decade later, Wemby did it. Now the giants rule the perimeter, with no end in sight.It was absurd, but the kind of absurd that he has normalized. And it was rewarded, tying the game and leading to an eventual win for the ages for the San Ant

item.title: When asked if watching Gilgeous-Alexander receive the …
item.description: When asked if watching Gilgeous-Alexander receive the trophy he wanted felt personal, Wemnanyama simply said yes, agreeing with the entirety of the premise. “We felt like that was his trophy,” Castle said. “I’m sure he felt the same way.” “One-hundred percent,” Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said. “To see another competitor with the trophy that he deserves — (SGA was) voted the winner. But if you’re a competitor and you see another competitor get rewarded with what you want — I don’t think it had any

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: 'Our effort should be over anybody else's and tonight we were relentless'
item.description: "Tonight almost felt a little bit symbolic, the MVP ceremony for SGA before the game and then you have a big response tonight with your performance. If your performance tonight had a message behind it, what would that message be?" Victor Wembanyama: "The message would be that we as a team are ready to go in any environment, in any place, against anybody, even though we still got a lot to learn. Our effort should be over anybody else's and tonight we were relentless."

item.title: “He’s a good player defensively, so he’s trying to …
item.description: But that amusement evaporated. Caruso kept shooting and hitting, often enough that it warranted Wembanyama gradually inching closer to him on the perimeter. He tried to weaponize the 22-year-old’s own overzealous tendencies. “He’s a good player defensively, so he’s trying to make all the plays, and rightfully so. He can make them,” Caruso said of Wembanyama. “So part of that is just being smart about how you attack him. You watch the first couple series that they played, and there’s times where

The Oklahoma City Thunder and San Antonio Spurs are just that good! They are talented, deep, athletic and both teams are well coached.

item.title: The world is 8 billion people, so it’s 8 billion …
item.description: Jared Weiss: Asked Victor Wembanyama if he feels he is the best basketball player in the world now: “Do I feel like it right now? I feel tired. But it’s not a question I’m wondering right now. We’ll see. The world is 8 billion people, so it’s 8 billion opinions.”

item.title: "It's obviously challenging -- very tall, very long, …
item.description: Wembanyama blocked three shots, including a midrange step-back jumper by Gilgeous-Alexander on the baseline midway through the fourth quarter, but that total hardly shows his defensive impact. His presence eliminated driving lanes and intimidated the Thunder's off-dribble creators, even the two-time MVP. "It's obviously challenging -- very tall, very long, deters a lot of things at the rim," Gilgeous-Alexander said. "You've got to be smart when you go in there. Be patient, but also be aggressive

item.title: "We're getting past the point of potential... that's …
item.description: Run It Back: DeMarcus Cousins says Victor Wembanyama is the real deal 💯 "We're getting past the point of potential... that's scary because he still has a lot of growing to do. ... Everything you want in a young franchise player, he's the perfect example of it."

item.title: In-demand Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney is …
item.description: In-demand Spurs assistant coach Sean Sweeney is another candidate expected to interview with the Magic, sources say, even though Sweeney is seeking his first head coaching post. Sweeney's first formal conversation with Orlando might have to be conducted remotely given Sweeney's Western Conference finals duties with San Antonio. League sources say Sweeney is also a prime candidate for Chicago's opening created by Donovan's departure.

item.title: “The league is in trouble for a very long time.”
item.description: Jared Weiss: Devin Vassell on the Spurs’ young talent: “The league is in trouble for a very long time.”

item.title: "We're just super connected on the court when it gets …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "We're just super connected on the court when it gets loud like that. You can't really hear fairly often but you know each other, you know what the next person is doing, what plays they're supposed to be running." -Vassell on executing in the road environment

item.title: "He'll do everything he can (to play), which is mostly …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch Johnson indicated Fox (sore right ankle) will be a game-time decision Wednesday night. "He'll do everything he can (to play), which is mostly rest and then get treatment to prepare for the next game, and tomorrow pregame we'll find out if he can," the #Spurs coach said.

item.title: “Everything is on the table. You can’t just throw one …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Mark Daigneault on what he saw when Chet Holmgren defended Victor Wembanyama during Game 1: “Everything is on the table. You can’t just throw one pitch throughout the whole series. We have reasons why we approached the game the way we did last night. Some of that was the regular-season matchups. Some of that was the stuff we saw in the first couple of rounds from ourselves and from them. But in these series, one of the things you’ve got to do is you’ve got to figure out what the

item.title: “A combination of things. I agree with that …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Mark Daigneault on what OKC did to keep San Antonio out of the paint early on in Game 1 and how it can replicate that for a full game tomorrow: “A combination of things. I agree with that assessment. When you’re looking at a game like that, you’re trying to show what’s sustainable for you and the opponent. Certainly, Wembanyama getting around the basket and being able to dunk the ball or us having to foul out of weakness to prevent a layup isn’t sustainable. The more you give th

item.title: De'Aaron Fox listed as questionable for Game 2
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: De’Aaron Fox is questionable tomorrow for Game 2.

item.title: Devin Vassell's 51 minutes last night were the most by …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Devin Vassell's 51 minutes last night were the most by any Spurs player since Danny Green played 52 in a triple OT game on Dec. 17, 2014. It also was only the second time in franchise history that 5 players played 44+ minutes in the same game with the other being Dec. 6, 1994.

item.title: The top candidates to replace Kidd were not …
item.description: The top candidates to replace Kidd were not immediately apparent Tuesday night, but San Antonio's Sean Sweeney and Minnesota's Micah Nori — widely regarded around the league as future head coaches — are both known quantities in Dallas.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama hiked dangerous route with 2,500-foot climb while dribbling a basketball during offseason training with monks
item.description: Once Wembanyama did, Master Yan'an said, he could challenge him to do something nobody else could do. One day he told Wembanyama to dribble a basketball up another dangerous mountain route to Sanhuangzhai, a monastery deep in the Song Mountains. The hike traversed cliffside plank paths, suspension bridges and ancient forests, and was five times as long as the one to Bodhidharma Cave. The trail forces you to climb roughly 2,500 feet in elevation across uneven ridges and stone. Master Yan'an said

item.title: "I needed time to find myself again and then to get …
item.description: The monastery and accommodations would be extremely basic -- a bed, or, in Wembanyama's case, three, and a dresser. The days would be extremely long -- six to eight hours per day of training, in addition to hours of meditation -- and challenging. Ndiaye relayed all of this to Wembanyama upon his return. Wembanyama was unfazed. He was intrigued by the discipline and challenge. By what it might unlock in him and what it might help him let go of. The blood clot in his arm had shaken him to his core

item.title: "I am always trying to think ahead and see how we can …
item.description: OVER THE PAST three decades, Ndiaye has represented some of the best players to come from France: Rudy Gobert, Nicolas Batum, Evan Fournier. He has known Wembanyama's family for almost as long. Wembanyama's mother, Elodie, even coached Ndiaye's son when he was just 5 years old. He always has understood the responsibility in representing a player with Wembanyama's natural athletic and mental gifts. But as Wembanyama has grown into his extraordinary talent, Ndiaye has also come to see it as a priv

item.title: "The way they think is different," Ndiaye told ESPN. …
item.description: Last April, he called Los Angeles Lakers general manager and president of basketball operations Rob Pelinka, who represented Kobe Bryant for much of his career, and scheduled a lunch. The reason was simple. "The way they think is different," Ndiaye told ESPN. "The way they play, the way they stretch themselves. Just their curiosity. How they study and watch things. They're both very creative on how to solve a problem."

item.title: "Basically he wanted to have a physical transformation …
item.description: "Basically he wanted to have a physical transformation so he can run forever and use his physical tools to get closer to the basket," Ndiaye said. "And then he wanted to be challenged." Mentally, physically, spiritually. Challenged in every way a supremely conditioned athlete who already pushes himself harder than most humans can be challenged. Pelinka listened while Ndiaye talked. He knew the type.

item.title: Ndiaye can't say if Wembanyama's nighttime trek up …
item.description: LEGEND HAS IT that Bodhidharma, the monk credited with bringing Zen Buddhism to China, climbed Wuru Peak in 527 A.D. and meditated in a cave near the top, facing a stone wall, for nine years. There were no steps back then, only a wild, mountainous trail. Over the past 1,500 years Shaolin monks have cleared the thick forest trail and laid stone steps so others can also make the pilgrimage. Ndiaye can't say if Wembanyama's nighttime trek up that peak is what led him to have the kind of night he di

item.title: "The way that he's behaving in terms of mental …
item.description: But he can say that his client looks and plays differently this season -- Wembanyama averaged three fewer 3-point attempts per game than last season -- more than ever before. He's moving differently, like a tiger. Like he has already arrived. "The way that he's behaving in terms of mental strength is something that I haven't seen before," Ndiaye said. "There's pressure in all these situations now. He has the awareness of that pressure and that he's an important piece of a team that is trying to

item.title: "No. 2, his conditioning is second to none. We're …
item.description: "No. 2, his conditioning is second to none. We're getting into that period of the year where you're playing every other day, and Victor is still looking so good physically, and I think this is the result of all this work. "He was running every day last year, every day. Running everywhere, always outside, up the walls in China, up the mountains, in the forests. When he got back to the States. He just ran everywhere, and he's so fast. No one can follow him."

item.title: The NBA announced details for the Spurs trip to Europe …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: The NBA announced details for the Spurs trip to Europe next season for 2 games vs. New Orleans. . The Paris game will be Jan. 14 at Accor Arena. The game in Manchester, England, will be Jan. 17 at Co-op Live. Additional info, including ticket sales, will be announced later.

item.title: Carmelo Anthony on Victor Wembanyama: 'This is on a whole different level than what we felt about LeBron James'
item.description: Carmelo Anthony on Victor Wembanyama: "He did, he caught a lob, he was standing on the ground, Chet was on his left arm, he just reached up and dunked. I've never seen nothing like that. And then just to add on to that, the three at the end of the game, you know how pressurized that situation is in that moment, to come down and shoot a transition three. And he came out after the game and said, 'I wasn't even thinking, I don't know what was going on.' That's a scary sign to me. For you to be doin

item.title: The Spurs and Pelicans will play in Europe next …
item.description: ‪Mike Vorkunov‬: The Spurs and Pelicans will play in Europe next season. A game in Paris on Thursday, Jan. 14, 2027, and at Co-op Live in Manchester, England, on Sunday, Jan. 17, 2027

item.title: They want to be in the Eastern Conference, but that's …
item.description: Darren Wolfson: "There is a ceiling on this roster. The Wolves realize that. Tim Connelly realized it last summer making a push for Kevin Durant. I foresee roster changes coming. Nothing has changed on my front. We've talked about this for multiple months. They are not firing the head coach. Chris Finch has multiple years, two years left on his contract. First off, Alex Rodriguez and Marc Lore are not cutting the big fat check to Chris Finch for millions of dollars to go away. But even so, those

item.title: The mentality, he cares about winning, it's not about …
item.description: DeMarcus Cousins on Victor Wembanyama: "Oh, he's special for a reason and, you know, he's still young in his career, but I think this kid has arrived now. I think we're getting past the point of potential. Like, we're actually seeing what this kid is going to be right now in the in this moment of his career. And that's scary because he still has a lot of growing to do. So, I mean, I think he's the perfect example of a young player coming in right now. The mentality, he cares about winning, it's

item.title: Chris Paul: I'm honestly not worried about Victor Wembanyama lasting
item.description: Chris Paul: "I'm honestly not worried about Vic lasting, right, playing for a long, a long time in the league because his preparation, the way he works, the way he trains, all the little details, he's that competitive. I told you, like when I got there, whether it's ping pong, whether it's chess, whether it's just shooting games after practice, and he's like a student of the game. Like he watches everything constantly and it's kind of crazy to see it all come together. Because I remember, I went

item.title: “[De’Aaron] Fox walked up to me and said, ‘I’m not …
item.description: Twenty-year-old Dylan Harper was in the San Antonio Spurs’ training room getting treatment before Game 1 of the NBA Western Conference finals Monday when a lineup scratch about 60 minutes before tipoff dramatically changed his role. Instead of coming off the bench as usual, the rookie suddenly had to prepare to make his first career playoff start on the road against the reigning NBA champion Oklahoma City Thunder. “[De’Aaron] Fox walked up to me and said, ‘I’m not playing,’” Harper told Andscape

item.title: Spurs head coach Mitch Johnson, speaking to the media …
item.description: “When I’m on the court, it’s all business. I’m just trying to prove to the world and to me that I belong,” Harper said. Spurs head coach Mitch Johnson, speaking to the media after Game 1, said of Harper: “I thought he was phenomenal. I thought he was even better defensively. It’s been rewarding as someone who has supported him this year.”

item.title: “It has been very rewarding for someone that has tried …
item.description: Harper is still playing and making history in the conference finals with the 62-win Spurs. “It has been very rewarding for someone that has tried to support him through this year and help him in terms of coaching him and giving him guidance of just trusting us,” Johnson said of Harper. “He was this good eight months ago. But we wanted him to get some corporate knowledge, learn the program and get some fundamentals of this league and how tough it was, to get in better shape. “He watched a lot of

item.title: “He told me to be me. That’s the biggest thing. He’s …
item.description: Harper said Fox gave him some words of wisdom before Game 1. “He told me to be me. That’s the biggest thing. He’s been that person I’ve looked up to in terms of how he plays, how he moves and everything. He told me to be me out there and not take it for granted,” the 6-foot-5 shooting guard said.

item.title: “There is stuff that Dylan does that you just can’t …
item.description: “There is stuff that Dylan does that you just can’t teach,” Spurs forward Harrison Barnes told Andscape. “The finishing package. The way he is able to be a point guard. To get a start today, seven steals; you just can’t teach that. But off the court, he’s a kid, one thousand percent. “He and [rookie forward] Carter Bryant are about the same age. And sometimes you might think Carter is the bigger brother. But I love Dylan. That is what allows him to be himself. He’s confident in who he is. He’s y

item.title: “It’s going to be a series, that’s for sure. We spoke …
item.description: What do you take away from Game 1? Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's trainer, Nem Ilic: “It’s going to be a series, that’s for sure. We spoke after the game. They went through it last year two times, both against Denver and Indiana. It’s funny how things work. Once another team wins the first game, then it’s all like, ‘Oh, are they good enough to take down San Antonio? Is [Nikola] Jokić going to beat Shai in the second round last year? Is Indiana going to win the Finals?’ I feel like it gets a little b

item.title: “I expect him to bounce back like he always has. …
item.description: What do you expect for Game 2? Ilic: “I expect him to bounce back like he always has. Obviously, San Antonio is a little bit of a different team. They have the ability to throw some different things at him. They have probably the greatest rim protector that we’ve seen. But I also think that Shai is good enough of a player that he will know how to make an adjustment to get more shots off and better shots. He still missed quite a bit in Game 1. I’m sure they made it a little bit tougher. But he’s

item.title: “They definitely have individual guys that are …
item.description: Compared to other opponents, what did you notice how San Antonio defended him and how Shai tried to handle it? Ilic: “They definitely have individual guys that are athletic enough to create some trouble. It’s a lot different when he does drive and you have Wemby down there protecting. Watching him in person for the first time, it changes everything. It’s a completely different game. You have to think about driving and what shot you’re going to throw up. That’s what gives the Spurs their unique a

item.title: De’Aaron Fox (ankle, questionable) is at shootaround, …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: De’Aaron Fox (ankle, questionable) is at shootaround, but he’s not getting up any shots with his teammates during the portion of the walkthrough open to media. Mitch Johnson said Tuesday he would be a game-time decision for Game 2.

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs are 36-3 since February 1 when …
item.description: Lev Akabas: The San Antonio Spurs are 36-3 since February 1 when Wemby has played at least 20 minutes... the losses: • 2 point loss to Wolves • 2 point loss to Nuggets • 25 point loss to Knicks

item.title: Jalen Williams exits Game 2 with treatment to hamstring
item.description: Tim MacMahon: Jalen Williams was getting treatment on his left hamstring after exiting. That’s the hamstring he strained in Game 2 of the first round, causing him to miss six games before coming back for the West finals opener.

item.title: Said this morning that he felt fresh returning from …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: No Jalen Williams since the first quarter. He’s been missing from the bench for a while. Said this morning that he felt fresh returning from his hamstring strain, after missing nearly a month, because he “hadn’t played all year.” Acknowledged that testing was extensive, because with these soft tissue injuries they constantly needed to check whether one leg was overcompensating for the other during recovery.

item.title: De'Aaron Fox out for Game 2
item.description: Shams Charania: Spurs' De'Aaron Fox is expected to miss his second consecutive contest in Game 2 tonight against the Oklahoma City Thunder due to a right high ankle sprain, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander leads Thunder in win to even up series against Spurs
item.description: Justin Martinez: Final: Thunder 122, Spurs 113 Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 30 points and 9 assists Isaiah Hartenstein: 10 points and 13 rebounds Alex Caruso: 17 points and 5 assists OKC ties the series at 1-1.

item.description: Justin Russo: Stephon Castle joins Sleepy Floyd, Cade Cunningham, and James Harden as the only players in league history to have 20 turnovers during any 2-game stretch. Castle the first to do it in the postseason.

item.title: Castle on his turnover woes: "I think it's more …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Castle on his turnover woes: "I think it's more personal on my end, just really speeding myself up, not allowing our screeners to get hits. So it's putting me in a position where I have to play fast or my screener might get an offensive foul. I put us at a disadvantage to start."

item.title: Stephon Castle has 20 turnovers in his last 2 games, …
item.description: ‪Keerthika Uthayakumar‬: Stephon Castle has 20 turnovers in his last 2 games, the most in any 2-game span in the playoffs since turnovers were first tracked in 1968.

item.title: Regarding Fox's bum ankle, he said: "He's just trying …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch Johnson had no update on Harper's leg injury. Regarding Fox's bum ankle, he said: "He's just trying to play every day. It's a tough injury that he wouldn't be playing with in the regular season, so he's trying to tough it out."
